description: Injection of malicious client-side scripts into vulnerable web application inputs.

complexity_factors.question: Was the payload stored in the database rather than reflected?
complexity_factors.reason: Stored XSS persists and affects every user who loads the page, making it harder to scope and contain than a one-time reflected attack.
complexity_factors.source: OWASP XSS community page — https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/xss/ — explicitly distinguishes stored XSS as higher impact and lower detectability than reflected XSS

complexity_factors.question: Did the payload execute in an admin or privileged user session?
complexity_factors.reason: Admin session hijacking grants attacker elevated access, expanding the blast radius and increasing containment effort.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1078 — Valid Accounts: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/ — abuse of privileged accounts increases impact severity and response complexity

complexity_factors.question: Was the payload encoded or obfuscated to bypass filters?
complexity_factors.reason: Obfuscation reduces detectability by WAF and signature-based tools, requiring more analyst effort to identify the malicious intent.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE T1027 — Obfuscated Files or Information: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1027/ — reduces detection confidence and increases analyst investigation time

complexity_factors.question: Was the XSS part of a larger attack chain (e.g. used to steal session and pivot)?
complexity_factors.reason: A chained attack means containment of XSS alone is insufficient — the analyst must trace and close all downstream exploitation paths.
complexity_factors.source: MITRE ATT&CK — Chained TTPs: https://attack.mitre.org/tactics/TA0002/ — chained techniques increase response complexity across multiple IR phases

complexity_factors.question: Was the XSS payload executed entirely client-side via DOM manipulation with no server-side reflection?
complexity_factors.reason: DOM-based XSS bypasses server-side WAF and log-based detection — the payload never reaches the server. Analysts must use browser-side forensics requiring higher skill and effort.
complexity_factors.source: OWASP DOM Based XSS — https://owasp.org/www-community/attacks/DOM_Based_XSS — defines DOM-based XSS as a variant where the attack executes via client-side script without server-side involvement, making standard server log detection ineffective

references.name: OWASP DOM-based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet

complexity_notes: Maximum possible score is 1+2+1+2+1=7. TTC ranges cover up to 8 for safety margin. dom_based_attack was reduced from adds:2 to adds:1 to keep the scale consistent with the original 1-6 design, since it is a detection difficulty modifier not a full escalation factor.

ttc_ranges.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 Section 3.3 — low severity incidents should be contained rapidly; MTTA benchmark: good SOC teams acknowledge in 6-10 min — https://kpidepot.com/kpi/mean-time-acknowledge-mtta

NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 Section 3.3 — medium severity; top SOC organizations average 10-60 min investigation — https://www.prophetsecurity.ai/blog/soc-metrics-that-matter-mttr-mtti-false-negatives-and-more
NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 Section 3.3 — high severity; IBM Cost of Data Breach 2024 — https://www.ibm.com/reports/data-breach — high complexity incidents take significantly longer to contain

rules.description: Alert must be investigated within 10 minutes of being raised
rules.trigger: No alert_investigation_started event within 600 seconds of alert_raised
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 Section 3.2.4 — initial analysis should begin promptly after detection; MTTA benchmark: 6-10 min is good, above 15 min is poor — https://kpidepot.com/kpi/mean-time-acknowledge-mtta

rules.description: Incident must be confirmed — blue team must escalate beyond initial alert
rules.trigger: No incident_confirmed event in event log
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 Section 3.2 — incident declaration is a required step in the IR lifecycle

rules.description: Containment must be initiated before max_tolerance threshold
rules.trigger: No containment_initiated event before max_tolerance_sec seconds
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 Section 3.3 — containment strategy must be selected and implemented promptly

rules.description: Containment must succeed — session terminated or input blocked
rules.source: NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 Section 3.3 — containment must be confirmed before incident can be closed

scenarios.label: Stored XSS — good response
scenarios.description: Blue team detects, investigates, confirms and contains a stored XSS alert within acceptable time.
scenarios.timing_source: MTTA: 6 min acknowledgment is good performance per https://kpidepot.com/kpi/mean-time-acknowledge-mtta; containment at 65 min is within acceptable range for complexity 3 per NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 Section 3.3

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31106 fired — XSS pattern detected in web log
Analyst-1 opened alert and began log review — 6 min MTTA (good)
Analyst-2 confirmed payload stored in DB, escalated to incident
Analyst-3 initiated WAF rule to block script tags in input
WAF rule active, payload stripped from stored records — 65 min total, within acceptable range

scenarios.label: Reflected XSS — alert dismissed incorrectly
scenarios.description: Blue team raises alert but analyst incorrectly dismisses it as false positive. Containment never happens. Auto-fail triggered by XSS-R04.
scenarios.timing_source: Auto-fail from XSS-R04 overrides score to 0 regardless of timing — NIST SP 800-61 Rev2 Section 3.3: containment must be confirmed

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31108 fired — encoded XSS pattern in query string
Analyst-1 marked alert as false positive — incorrect decision, no containment follows

scenarios.label: DOM-Based XSS — delayed detection, successful containment
scenarios.description: Wazuh fires a CSP violation alert. Blue team struggles initially since no malicious payload appears in server logs. Eventually confirms DOM-based XSS and deploys CSP fix.
scenarios.timing_source: MTTA: 10.8 min acknowledgment is delayed; containment at 51.6 min is before max_tolerance of 55 min for complexity 2 — XSS-R01 penalty applied, no XSS-R03 penalty
notes: DOM-based XSS payload operates entirely in the browser — server logs show no injected script.
Detection relies on CSP violation reports rather than HTTP request inspection.
Containment achieved via Content Security Policy hardening and replacing vulnerable innerHTML sink with textContent.

references.name: OWASP DOM-based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet

logs.detail: Wazuh rule 31110 fired — CSP violation report received; script-src policy blocked inline execution from document.location sink
Analyst-1 opened alert — 10.8 min MTTA (delayed); server request logs reviewed — no malicious payload found in HTTP body or query string
Analyst-2 reviewed CSP violation report — confirmed DOM-based XSS via document.location fragment injection into innerHTML sink
Analyst-3 deployed updated Content Security Policy and replaced vulnerable innerHTML sink with textContent
CSP policy active and sink patched — no further CSP violation reports; DOM-based XSS vector eliminated
